metadata.note: with_skill = iteration-2 improved skill (context-cost aware, tiered references, single next step); without_skill = iteration-1 new_skill baseline

expectations.evidence: States repository pattern directly, doesn't just point to user_repo.go
One paragraph: create schema + implement middleware

notes: 39 lines. Single Next Step paragraph. 2 read-on-resume files. Tiered References structure.

expectations.evidence: Line 32: 'Repository pattern: QueryRowContext for single row, Scan into struct'
FAILED: Has '## Action Items & Next Steps' with 4 numbered items (create schema, implement middleware, implement PUT, wire routes). Not a single focused direction.

notes: 44 lines. 4 numbered action items instead of single next step. Section names are 'Critical Context'/'Working Set' not tiered 'References'.

expectations.evidence: 3 labeled bullets: deadlock constraint, root cause, why previous fix failed
'sync.Map (simplest, no lock ordering issues)' with validation command

notes: 32 lines — most concise across all iterations. Only 1 read-on-resume file (debug-notes.md) vs iter-1's 3 files. Deadlock knowledge fully inlined.
40 lines. Already had single next step. But 3 read-on-resume files (handler, dispatcher, debug-notes) vs iter-2's 1 file — the improved skill taught inlining the handler/dispatcher knowledge.

expectations.evidence: Explains bug implication: 'stale entry persists until next read-miss. This is a bug'
Single paragraph: wire invalidation into UpdateProduct
Code Review Feedback and Cache Invalidation labeled sections

notes: 45 lines. Single Next Step. Only 1 read-on-resume file. Learnings explains the bug implication, not just the pattern name.

expectations.evidence: Line 36: 'Cache write-through pattern means invalidation must happen on write operations'
FAILED: Has '## Action Items & Next Steps' with 4 numbered items. Not a single focused direction.

notes: 48 lines. 4 numbered action items instead of single Next Step. Learnings explains pattern but not the bug implication (that stale cache entries persist).
Iteration-2 scores 100% pass rate vs iteration-1's 93.3% — the discriminating assertion is 'single clear Next Step' which iter-1 fails on 2/3 evals (mid-feature: 4 items, multi-concern: 4 items)

Read-on-resume file count dropped from 2.3 avg to 1.3 avg (43% reduction) — the context-cost awareness guidance in the improved skill is working. The debugging eval showed the biggest improvement: 3 files → 1 file

The debugging eval's iter-2 output (32 lines) is the most concise handoff across all iterations. It inlines the complete deadlock explanation rather than listing handler/dispatcher as required reading
Line count decreased from 44.0 avg to 38.7 avg (12% reduction) despite the improved skill actually asking for MORE inline knowledge — the savings come from eliminating the action-items list pattern

Learnings sections in iter-2 consistently explain implications (e.g., 'stale entry persists until next read-miss — this is a bug') rather than just naming patterns ('cache uses write-through'). The skill's guidance to 'state facts in the document body' is producing deeper inline knowledge

All iter-2 outputs use the new unified 'References' section with 'Read on resume' / 'Touch during execution' tiers, replacing the old 'Critical Context' / 'Working Set' split. This directly maps to the resume-handoff skill's loading layers

The debugging eval passes all assertions in both iterations (10/10), making it non-discriminating for pass_rate. However, the read-on-resume reduction (3 → 1) is a real quality improvement not captured by current assertions
